blocks.body: Future Forms: Choosing the Right Form helps you quickly choose between will, be going to, Future Continuous, Future Perfect, and Future Perfect Continuous depending on whether you mean a decision, a ready plan, visible evidence, an action in progress, a completed result, or duration up to a future point.

{"level":"B1–B2","intro":"On this page, you compare all five future forms and choose the form by meaning, not only by the word tomorrow: a decision now, a ready plan, visible evidence, an action in progress, a completed result, or duration before a future limit.","rules":[{"label":"WILL","color":"blue","text":"will + V1 for a spontaneous decision, promise, offer, or neutral prediction.","example":"I'll answer it. / I think it will be fine."},{"label":"BE GOING TO","color":"emerald","text":"be going to + V1 for an already decided plan, intention, or a prediction based on evidence.","example":"We're going to move in June. / Look at that sky. It's going to rain."},{"label":"FUTURE CONTINUOUS","color":"amber","text":"will be + V-ing for an action that will be in progress at a specific future moment.","example":"At 8 p.m., I'll be studying."},{"label":"FUTURE PERFECT","color":"violet","text":"will have + V3 for a result that will already be complete by a deadline or future point.","example":"By 8 p.m., I'll have finished the report."},{"label":"FUTURE PERFECT CONTINUOUS","color":"rose","text":"will have been + V-ing for duration or process up to a future point.","example":"By 8 p.m., I'll have been studying for two hours."}]}

{"title":"1. Quick decision guide","items":["Do you need a spontaneous reaction, promise, or neutral prediction? → choose will + V1.","Was the plan or intention already decided earlier, or can you see evidence now? → choose be going to + V1.","Will the action still be in progress at a future moment? → choose will be + V-ing.","Is a completed result important by the deadline? → choose will have + V3.","Is duration or the question how long? important before a future point? → choose will have been + V-ing."]}

{"title":"2. All-forms comparison matrix","intro":"Look not only at future time, but at what exactly you want to focus on:","rows":[{"en":"will + V1","ua":"decision now, promise, offer, neutral prediction","note":"I'll call her now. / I think they'll win."},{"en":"be going to + V1","ua":"ready plan, intention, or prediction with visible evidence","note":"We're going to visit Rome. / Look at the clouds. It's going to rain."},{"en":"will be + V-ing","ua":"action in progress at a future point","note":"At 9 tomorrow, I'll be taking the test."},{"en":"will have + V3","ua":"completed result before a deadline","note":"By 10, I'll have finished the test."},{"en":"will have been + V-ing","ua":"duration or process up to a future point","note":"By 10, I'll have been studying for three hours."}],"warning":"💡 If two forms seem possible, ask yourself whether you need a decision / plan / evidence or a process / completed result / duration before the deadline."}

{"title":"3. Common scenarios with paired examples","sections":[{"label":"Decision now vs ready plan","color":"blue","description":"The same future action sounds different when the decision appears now or already existed before the conversation.","examples":[{"en":"The phone is ringing. I'll answer it.","ua":"The decision appears at the moment of speaking → will."},{"en":"I already know. I'm going to call her tonight.","ua":"The plan existed before the conversation → be going to."},{"en":"Don't worry, I'll help you.","ua":"A short promise or offer → will."}]},{"label":"Opinion vs visible evidence","color":"emerald","description":"For an opinion or neutral prediction, we often choose will; for a prediction with visible proof, we often choose be going to.","examples":[{"en":"I think they'll win.","ua":"This is the speaker's opinion → will."},{"en":"Look at the score. They're going to win.","ua":"There is visible evidence now → be going to."}]},{"label":"Future point, deadline, and duration","color":"violet","description":"Once the event is clearly in the future, the next choice depends on focus: process at that point, completed result, or duration before the limit.","examples":[{"en":"At 9 tomorrow, I'll be taking the test.","ua":"What will be happening then? → Future Continuous."},{"en":"By 10, I'll have finished the test.","ua":"What will already be ready by the deadline? → Future Perfect."},{"en":"By 10, I'll have been taking practice tests for three hours.","ua":"How long will the process already have lasted by that point? → Future Perfect Continuous."}],"note":"📌 If your question sounds like what will be happening then?, look toward will be + V-ing. If it sounds like what will already be finished by then?, look toward will have + V3. If it sounds like how long will it have been happening by then?, look toward will have been + V-ing."}]}

{"title":"4. Typical mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"blue","title":"Using will for a plan that was decided earlier","wrong":"❌ I decided yesterday. I'll visit my aunt this weekend.","right":"✅ I decided yesterday. I'm going to visit my aunt this weekend."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"emerald","title":"Using be going to for a spontaneous promise right now","wrong":"❌ I'm going to help you with those bags.","right":"✅ I'll help you with those bags."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"violet","title":"Using Future Continuous when a completed result is needed by the deadline","wrong":"❌ By 6 p.m., I'll be finishing the report.","right":"✅ By 6 p.m., I'll have finished the report."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"rose","title":"Forgetting been or the idea of duration in Future Perfect Continuous","wrong":"❌ By July, I'll have working here for a year.","right":"✅ By July, I'll have been working here for a year."}]}

{"title":"5. Ask yourself before you choose","items":["Is this a decision right now or a ready plan?","Am I giving an opinion or reacting to visible evidence?","Do I need to show that the action will be in progress at a future moment?","Do I need to show that the result will already be finished by the deadline?","Do I need to show how long the process will already have been continuing by that future point?"]}

{"title":"6. Pages and related topics in this block","items":[{"label":"Future Forms: Choosing the Right Form","url":"/en/theory/maibutni-formy/choosing-the-right-future-form","current":true},{"label":"Will vs Be Going To","url":"/en/theory/maibutni-formy/will-vs-be-going-to","current":false},{"label":"Future Perfect vs Future Continuous","url":"/en/theory/maibutni-formy/future-perfect-vs-future-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Future Perfect vs Future Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/maibutni-formy/future-perfect-vs-future-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Future Continuous vs Future Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/maibutni-formy/future-continuous-vs-future-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Future Continuous","url":"/en/theory/future-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Future Perfect","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect","current":false},{"label":"Future Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous","current":false}]}
